headline: MITI's Trade Agreements Cover 64% of Commerce But SMEs Can't Access
context: Malaysia has 18 active free trade agreements covering 64% of its total trade, but a 2025 MITI study found that only 28% of eligible SME exports actually utilised preferential tariff rates due to complex rules-of-origin compliance requirements.

cards.big: 18 trade agreements but 72% of eligible SME exports pay full tariffs anyway — FTAs work for big companies, not small ones
cards.sub: Rules-of-origin documentation costs RM8,000-RM15,000 per shipment — prohibitive for SMEs exporting below RM500,000

cards.big: RM4.8 billion in preferential tariff savings goes unclaimed annually by Malaysian SMEs due to compliance complexity
cards.sub: Large manufacturers capture 84% of FTA benefits — 12,000 SME exporters effectively compete without the advantages their country negotiated

cards.big: MATRADE's FTA training programme reached only 4,200 SMEs in 2025 — barely 3% of the 140,000 SMEs engaged in cross-border trade
cards.sub: Training delivered in English — 68% of Malay-medium SME owners cited language barrier as primary obstacle to FTA utilisation

cards.big: FTA utilisation rates tracked by MITI rely on Customs data sampling — no real-time dashboard shows which SMEs claim which benefits
cards.sub: RCEP took effect in 2022 but 3 years later, MITI cannot report how many Malaysian companies have used RCEP preferential rates

cards.big: Negotiating 18 trade deals that mostly benefit big corporations isn't trade diplomacy for the nation — it's free trade for the few
Should MITI create a one-stop FTA compliance portal that auto-generates rules-of-origin certificates for qualifying SME exports?
